TimeEntry.Title: 3cx upgrade from 16 to v18
TimeEntry.Notes: Took the configuration Full backup of the server. Placed in a different location than the default. Downloaded the v18 setup from the 3cx portal. Uninstalled v16 and restarted the server. Install

TimeEntry.Title: RE: 3CX maintenance agreement
TimeEntry.Notes: Connected the Device with the network, Run an advanced IP scanner and got the IP, logged in from GUI, and successfully registered the device

TimeEntry.Title: RE: 3CX maintenance agreement
TimeEntry.Notes: Requested Hamid to attach the Device in-network, run advanced IP scanner, got the IP and logged in GUI. made some changes, and registered the device successfully. requested James outbound and inbound,

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Rate for South Korea
TimeEntry.Notes: Called support and work with them to setup an international calls. Called client and test the international calls to South Korea and other USA numbers as well. All are working fine now.

TimeEntry.Title: Re: FW: 3CX maintenance agreement
TimeEntry.Notes: Called support multiple times to setup fax for the client and assist client as per support guideline and try to troubleshoot and fix the issue.

TimeEntry.Title: Contacting AT&T support for USFI call issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Contacting AT&T support for USFI call issue, due to issue with incoming call flow, I was on hold for over 45 mins but then got a hold of support & they verified all the details and mentioned t

TimeEntry.Title: Contacting AT&T support for USFI call issue
TimeEntry.Notes: Sinch voice agent called in and informed us that they are carriers but not the ones who sold the number to client so need to verify with client who their ISP or phone number provider is.

TimeEntry.Title: 3CX call issue for USFI
TimeEntry.Notes: Logged into AT&T Router and verified once again all the ports, made some changes in 3cx, Server rebooted and Router, installed wireshark and traced the calls Logs, Found something wrong on Router

TimeEntry.Title: FW: International Call from US to CYPRUS
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per the user’s request, they needed assistance setting up international calling to Cyprus. After reviewing the request, I discussed the details with Ravi for confirmation. It was verified that the country code +971 belongs to the United Arab Emirates (UAE), while Cyprus uses +357.

A reply was sent to the user clarifying the correct country code and requesting confirmation of the desired destination and required monthly international calling minutes. Awaiting user confirmation to proceed with SIP configuration and rate setup.

TimeEntry.Title: 3CX PHONE OUT OF SERVICE
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Title: 3CX Phones Out of Service – USFI Foods

Summary:
User James Cho (USFI Foods) reported that all desktop 3CX phones were showing red lights and had been out of service since the weekend.

Action Taken:

  • Sent remote session setup instructions via email, but the user was unable to establish the session.

  • During the follow-up call, established a session using Quick Assist.

  • While troubleshooting, requested access to the 3CX server to investigate further; however, the user did not have access to the server room.

  • The user connected us with the on-site IT personnel for assistance.

  • Upon further investigation, determined that ISP services were down due to an AT&T outage.

Resolution:
Confirmed the issue was caused by an ISP outage. No faults found on the 3CX system. The user, James Cho, will contact us again once the ISP connection is restored if the issue persists.

Status: Closed – Root cause identified as ISP outage (AT&T).

TimeEntry.Title: FW: 3CX | Upcoming renewal of your phone system subscription
TimeEntry.Notes: The license has been renewed for the client.
